$comment: Part info database, keyed by mesh name. Keys are exact mesh names or prefixes ending in '*' (e.g. 'Int_Piston_*' covers Int_Piston_1..12; the longest matching pattern wins). Add entries here — no code changes needed. Fields: name, system, function, description.

Body_Hood.name: Hood (Bonnet)
Body_Hood.function: Covers and protects the engine bay while allowing service access.
Body_Hood.description: Aluminium panel on the W221 to cut front-axle weight. Houses insulation to damp V12 intake noise and channels crash energy in a frontal impact.

Body_Grille.name: Radiator Grille
Body_Grille.function: Feeds ambient air to the radiator stack and both intercooler circuits.
Body_Grille.description: The S65 AMG grille sits ahead of an unusually deep cooler pack: engine radiator, ATF cooler, power steering/ABC cooler, and the low-temperature radiator for the water-to-air charge cooling.

Chassis_Floorpan.name: Floorpan / Unibody Floor
Chassis_Floorpan.function: Primary structural floor tying front and rear crash structures together.
Chassis_Floorpan.description: The W221 uses a steel unibody with aluminium closures. The floorpan carries seat mounts, tunnel (driveshaft + exhaust routing) and the fuel tank saddle.

Susp_ABCStrut_*.name: Active Body Control (ABC) Strut
Susp_ABCStrut_*.function: Hydraulically adjusts spring preload at each corner to counter roll, pitch and dive in milliseconds.
Susp_ABCStrut_*.description: The S65 uses ABC rather than Airmatic: a tandem pump driven by the engine pressurises the system to ~200 bar. Each strut pairs a coil spring with a hydraulic plunger controlled by valve blocks. A common failure point — watch for fluid leaks at the strut heads.

Susp_Tire_*.name: Tyre
Susp_Tire_*.function: The only contact patch with the road — transmits drive, braking and cornering forces.
Susp_Tire_*.description: S65 AMG runs staggered fitments (255 front / 275 rear on 19–20" wheels). With 1,000 Nm on tap, rear tyre life is famously short — inspect inner shoulders for wear.

Susp_Rim_*.name: Wheel (Forged Rim)
Susp_Rim_*.function: Carries the tyre and transfers loads between hub and road.
Susp_Rim_*.description: Forged multi-spoke AMG wheels to cut unsprung mass — a key concern given how hard the ABC hydraulics work over bumps.

Susp_BrakeRotor_*.name: Brake Rotor (Compound Disc)
Susp_BrakeRotor_*.function: Converts kinetic energy to heat when clamped by the caliper.
Susp_BrakeRotor_*.description: 390 mm two-piece compound rotors up front: cast iron friction ring on an aluminium hat, allowing thermal expansion without coning.

Susp_BrakeCaliper_*.name: Brake Caliper
Susp_BrakeCaliper_*.function: Squeezes the pads against the rotor under hydraulic pressure.
Susp_BrakeCaliper_*.description: Fixed multi-piston AMG calipers. Front axle uses 8-piston units on the S65 — pad wear sensors report to the instrument cluster.

Susp_ControlArm_*.name: Control Arms (Wishbones)
Susp_ControlArm_*.function: Locate the wheel hub while letting it travel vertically.
Susp_ControlArm_*.description: Forged aluminium arms with rubber-hydro bushings. Worn front lower bushings cause the classic W221 brake-shimmy complaint.

Harness_Main.name: Main Body Harness
Harness_Main.function: Trunk-to-front power and CAN-bus backbone linking body controllers.
Harness_Main.description: The W221 runs multiple CAN buses (engine, interior, telematics) plus MOST fibre for infotainment. The main harness routes along the right sill from the trunk-mounted battery to the front SAM (signal acquisition module).

Harness_Battery.name: Battery (Trunk-mounted)
Harness_Battery.function: Supplies cranking power and stabilises the 12 V system.
Harness_Battery.description: Mounted in the trunk for weight distribution and heat isolation. The S65 additionally has an auxiliary battery for backup of shift-by-wire and consumers during ECO start/stop-less idle loads.

Chassis_Driveshaft.name: Propeller Shaft (Driveshaft)
Chassis_Driveshaft.function: Carries torque from the transmission to the rear differential.
Chassis_Driveshaft.description: Two-piece steel shaft with a centre support bearing and flex discs at each end. The flex discs (hardy discs) are wear items — cracks cause driveline clunk.

Chassis_Differential.name: Rear Differential & Axle Shafts
Chassis_Differential.function: Splits torque between the rear wheels while allowing them to rotate at different speeds.
Chassis_Differential.description: Strengthened rear axle centre on the S65 with a shorter final drive than lesser W221s. Axle shafts use CV joints at both ends.

Chassis_FuelTank.name: Fuel Tank
Chassis_FuelTank.function: Stores 90 L of fuel; houses the in-tank pump and level senders.
Chassis_FuelTank.description: Saddle-shaped tank over the rear axle with a jet pump transferring fuel between halves. The V12 at full boost can empty it in well under 200 km.

Chassis_Exhaust_*.name: Exhaust System (one side)
Chassis_Exhaust_*.function: Carries exhaust from the downpipes through catalysts and mufflers to the rear.
Chassis_Exhaust_*.description: True dual system, one path per bank. The S65 uses less restrictive AMG mufflers — most of the V12's distant-thunder sound signature is created here.

Chassis_Radiator.name: Cooling Pack (Radiator)
Chassis_Radiator.function: Rejects engine heat to ambient air.
Chassis_Radiator.description: The S65 nose stacks the engine radiator with the ATF cooler, the ABC/power-steering cooler and the low-temperature radiator for charge cooling — airflow management here is critical.

Chassis_SteeringRack.name: Steering Rack
Chassis_SteeringRack.function: Converts steering wheel rotation into tie-rod movement.
Chassis_SteeringRack.description: Hydraulic rack-and-pinion, powered by the same tandem pump that pressurises ABC. Speed-sensitive assist via the parameter steering valve.

Harness_FuseBox_EngineBay.name: Engine Bay Fuse & Relay Box (Front SAM)
Harness_FuseBox_EngineBay.function: Distributes fused power to engine-bay consumers and reads front sensors.
Harness_FuseBox_EngineBay.description: The front Signal Acquisition Module combines fuses, relays and a CAN node. First stop when chasing dead circuits at the front of the car.

Body_GrilleLouvres.name: Grille Louvres (Chrome)
Body_GrilleLouvres.function: Horizontal chrome bars of the radiator grille.
Body_GrilleLouvres.description: The W221 S65 wears a three-louvre chrome grille — one of the fastest ways to tell an S65 from lesser W221s (which use more, thinner louvres).

Body_BumperIntake_*.name: Bumper Air Intake
Body_BumperIntake_*.function: Feeds air to the intercooler low-temp radiator, oil coolers and front brakes.
Body_BumperIntake_*.description: The S65 front apron has enlarged intakes — the V12's cooling stack needs far more airflow than the V8 cars, especially with ABC and A/C condensers sharing the nose.

Body_Interior*.name: Cabin / Interior
Body_Interior*.function: Seating, controls and trim for driver and passengers.
Body_Interior*.description: S65s came near-fully optioned: ventilated massage seats, Exclusive nappa leather, Alcantara headliner. The interior lifts out with the body shell in this model.

Chassis_ExhaustTips_*.name: Quad Exhaust Tips
Chassis_ExhaustTips_*.function: Finishers where the twin pipes exit each side of the rear apron.
Chassis_ExhaustTips_*.description: Chromed twin oval tips per side — the S65's quad-tip signature. AMG V12 cars got oval tips where V8 AMGs of the era wore squared ones.

Body_Headlight_*.name: Headlamp (Bi-Xenon)
Body_Headlight_*.function: Active bi-xenon low/high beam with cornering light.
Body_Headlight_*.description: W221s use the Intelligent Light System: the projector swivels with steering angle and adapts its pattern to speed. Ballast failures are the usual fault.

Body_Taillight_*.name: Tail Lamp
Body_Taillight_*.function: Tail, brake, indicator and reverse functions in one LED cluster.
Body_Taillight_*.description: Facelift W221s switched to full LED clusters with two-stage brake-light intensity (brighter under hard braking).

Body_Mirror_*.name: Door Mirror
Body_Mirror_*.function: Rear view; houses indicator repeater and blind-spot radar warning LED.
Body_Mirror_*.description: Power-folding, auto-dimming, heated, with memory. The blind-spot warning triangle lights up in the glass.

Body_Glass_Side_*.name: Side Glass (Greenhouse)
Body_Glass_Side_*.function: Side visibility; part of the acoustic insulation package.
Body_Glass_Side_*.description: Double-laminated acoustic glass on the S-Class — a big part of why the V12 is nearly inaudible at cruise.

Body_Pillars_*.name: Roof Pillars (A/B/C)
Body_Pillars_*.function: Carry roof loads and form the passenger safety cell.
Body_Pillars_*.description: High-strength steel pillars; the B-pillar is the main side-impact load path and anchors the front seatbelts.

Harness_ECU.name: Engine Control Unit (ME 2.7.1)
Harness_ECU.function: Runs fuelling, ignition, boost control and torque management for all 12 cylinders.
Harness_ECU.description: The ME ECU coordinates with the transmission and ESP over CAN to cap torque in lower gears — the famous 1,000 Nm is itself an electronic limit.

EngineProxy_Block.name: M275 Engine (in bay)
EngineProxy_Block.function: Placeholder engine shown in the bay — use Focus Engine to study it in detail.
EngineProxy_Block.description: The engine sits longitudinally, set far back against the firewall for weight balance. Switch to the engine focus view to peel its layers.

EngineProxy_Transmission.name: 5G-Tronic Transmission (W5A 780)
EngineProxy_Transmission.function: Five-speed automatic transferring up to 1000 Nm to the rear axle.
EngineProxy_Transmission.description: The S65 keeps the older 5-speed because the 7G-Tronic of the era couldn't take the V12's torque. The W5A 780 is the strengthened version rated for AMG duty.

sw221_body_sedan*.name: Body Shell (Unibody)
sw221_body_sedan*.function: The welded steel structure everything else bolts to.
sw221_body_sedan*.description: W221 unibody with aluminium hood, trunk lid and front fenders. Torsional stiffness rose ~25% over the W220 — the foundation that lets ABC work precisely.

sw221_hood_*.name: Hood (Bonnet)
sw221_hood_*.function: Covers the engine bay; service access to the V12.
sw221_hood_*.description: Aluminium panel with the facelift power dome. Under it, the M275 fills the bay so completely that even spark-plug access means removing the charge-air ducting.

sw221_door_*.name: Door
sw221_door_*.function: Entry/exit; carries glass, side-impact protection and the door module.
sw221_door_*.description: Double-sealed doors with optional soft-close. Side-impact beams and pressure sensors for the side airbags live inside.

sw221_doorglass_*.name: Door Glass
sw221_doorglass_*.function: Side visibility; part of the acoustic package.
sw221_doorglass_*.description: Double-laminated acoustic glass — a big part of why the V12 is nearly inaudible at cruise.

sw221_windshield*.name: Windshield
sw221_windshield*.function: Forward visibility; bonded in as a structural member.
sw221_windshield*.description: Laminated, acoustically damped, with the rain/light sensor at the mirror mount. Bonded glass contributes measurably to body stiffness.

sw221_backlight*.name: Rear Window
sw221_backlight*.function: Rear visibility; carries the defroster grid and antennas.
sw221_backlight*.description: The FM diversity and remote-key antennas are printed into the defroster grid — scratched grids cause real reception faults.

sw221_bumper_F*.name: Front Bumper (AMG)
sw221_bumper_F*.function: Impact absorption, cooling airflow management, sensor carrier.
sw221_bumper_F*.description: Facelift AMG apron with enlarged intakes for the S65's cooling stack. Houses the Parktronic sensors; behind it sits an aluminium crash member.

sw221_bumper_R*.name: Rear Bumper (AMG)
sw221_bumper_R*.function: Rear impact absorption; frames the quad exhaust tips.
sw221_bumper_R*.description: AMG rear apron with cutouts for the twin oval tips each side — the giveaway that a V12 lives up front.

sw221_fender_*.name: Front Fender
sw221_fender_*.description: Aluminium on the W221. The S65 wears its 'V12 BITURBO' badges here, just behind the arch.

sw221_sideskirts*.name: Side Skirts (AMG)
sw221_sideskirts*.function: Visually lowers the car and manages airflow along the sills.
sw221_sideskirts*.description: Part of the AMG body kit, painted body colour on the S65.

sw221_tailgate*.name: Trunk Lid
sw221_tailgate*.function: Cargo access; power closing on the S65.
sw221_tailgate*.description: Aluminium lid with hydraulic power closing, carrying the S 65 AMG lettering.

sw221_wipers*.name: Wipers
sw221_wipers*.description: The park position lifts slightly off-glass to protect the rubber during heat soak.

sw221_badge*.name: Badging
sw221_badge*.description: Hood star and 'V12 BITURBO' fender badges. Enthusiasts check these against the VIN — badge swaps are common on lesser W221s.

sw221_lettering*.name: Trunk Lettering
sw221_lettering*.function: Model designation on the trunk lid.
sw221_lettering*.description: S 65 AMG — spacing and font are model-year specific, another authenticity check.

sw221_mirror*.name: Door Mirror
sw221_mirror*.function: Rear view; houses indicator repeater and blind-spot warning.
sw221_mirror*.description: Power-folding, auto-dimming, heated, with memory. The blind-spot warning triangle lights in the glass.

sw221_headlight*.name: Headlamp (Bi-Xenon ILS)
sw221_headlight*.function: Active bi-xenon low/high beam with cornering light.
sw221_headlight*.description: Intelligent Light System: the projector swivels with steering angle and adapts its pattern to speed. Ballast failures are the usual fault.

sw221_taillight*.name: Tail Lamp
sw221_taillight*.function: Tail, brake, indicator and reverse functions.
sw221_taillight*.description: Facelift LED clusters with two-stage brake-light intensity — flashing under emergency braking.

sw221_dash*.name: Dashboard
sw221_dash*.function: Instrument panel, COMAND display, HVAC outlets, passenger airbag.
sw221_dash*.description: The W221 dash introduced the COMAND controller-and-screen layout Mercedes kept for a decade. Behind it runs the main interior harness and the cabin CAN gateway.

sw221_decals*.name: Instrument Cluster
sw221_decals*.description: On the S65 the 360 km/h speedometer is one of the subtle flexes. The cluster is just another CAN node — if it dies, the car still drives.

sw221_needle_*.name: Gauge Needle
sw221_needle_*.description: The needles sweep full-scale on startup as a self-test.

sw221_seat*.name: Seats
sw221_seat*.function: Seating with ventilation, massage and dynamic bolsters.
sw221_seat*.description: Ventilated massage multicontour seats in Exclusive nappa. Each front seat has its own control module and weighs as much as an engine block.

sw221_steer*.name: Steering Wheel
sw221_steer*.function: Driver input; shift paddles and multifunction controls.
sw221_steer*.description: AMG wheel with aluminium paddles for the 5G-Tronic. The driver airbag and clock spring live behind it.

sw221_doorpanel_*.name: Door Panel (Interior)
sw221_doorpanel_*.function: Interior trim; window/mirror/seat controls, speaker carrier.
sw221_doorpanel_*.description: Wood and nappa over the door module; the harman/kardon mid-woofers mount here.

sw221_intmirror*.name: Interior Mirror
sw221_intmirror*.description: Electrochromic auto-dim; Distronic cars hide extra electronics in the mount.

sw221_chassis*.name: Underbody / Floorpan
sw221_chassis*.function: Structural floor tying front and rear crash structures together.
sw221_chassis*.description: Steel unibody floor with the transmission tunnel routing driveshaft and exhaust. Seat mounts, tank saddle and jacking points all land here.

sw221_subframe*.name: Subframe
sw221_subframe*.function: Carries suspension and drivetrain loads into the body through rubber mounts.
sw221_subframe*.description: The front subframe carries the engine and lower arms; the rear carries the differential and multi-link. Worn subframe bushings blur the whole car's responses.

sw221_lowerarm*.name: Lower Control Arm
sw221_lowerarm*.function: Locates the wheel carrier at its lower pivot.
sw221_lowerarm*.description: Forged aluminium. Front lower bushings are the classic W221 wear item — brake shimmy is the symptom.

sw221_upperarm*.name: Upper Control Arm
sw221_upperarm*.function: Locates the wheel carrier at its upper pivot.
sw221_upperarm*.description: Part of the rear multi-link — five links per rear corner control toe, camber and thrust independently.

sw221_strutbrace*.name: Strut Brace
sw221_strutbrace*.function: Ties the strut towers together to stiffen the front end.
sw221_strutbrace*.description: Reduces tower flex under cornering loads — buys steering precision, not power.

sw221_strut_F*.name: ABC Strut (Front)
sw221_strut_F*.function: Hydraulically adjusts spring preload to counter roll, pitch and dive in milliseconds.
sw221_strut_F*.description: Active Body Control: ~200 bar from the engine-driven tandem pump, valve blocks per axle. Leaks at the strut heads are the known failure — the car sags overnight when they go.

sw221_spring_R*.name: Spring (Rear ABC)
sw221_spring_R*.function: Carries the corner's static load; the ABC plunger adjusts its preload.
sw221_spring_R*.description: The coil does the steady-state work so the hydraulics only handle dynamics — key to ABC's response speed.

sw221_shock_R*.name: Damper (Rear)
sw221_shock_R*.description: Integrated into the rear ABC strut assembly on the S65.

sw221_swaybar*.name: Anti-roll Bar
sw221_swaybar*.function: Couples left and right wheels to resist body roll.
sw221_swaybar*.description: Thinner than you'd expect on an ABC car — the hydraulics do most of the roll control, the bars just trim balance.

sw221_tierod*.name: Tie Rod
sw221_tierod*.function: Connects the steering rack to the wheel carrier.
sw221_tierod*.description: Adjustable for toe during alignment. Play here shows up as vague on-centre feel.

sw221_hub*.name: Wheel Hub & Carrier
sw221_hub*.function: Carries the wheel bearing, brake disc and wheel.
sw221_hub*.description: The knuckle every arm, rod and strut meets. Wheel-speed sensors read a magnetic encoder in the bearing seal.

sw221_brakedisc*.name: Brake Disc (AMG Compound)
sw221_brakedisc*.function: Converts kinetic energy to heat when clamped by the caliper.
sw221_brakedisc*.description: 390 mm two-piece compound front rotors: cast iron friction ring on an aluminium hat, allowing heat expansion without coning.

sw221_caliper*.name: Brake Caliper (AMG)
sw221_caliper*.function: Clamps the pads against the disc under hydraulic pressure.
sw221_caliper*.description: Fixed multi-piston AMG calipers; pad-wear sensors report to the cluster.

sw221_steeringbox*.name: Steering Rack
sw221_steeringbox*.function: Converts steering wheel rotation into tie-rod movement.
sw221_steeringbox*.description: Hydraulic rack-and-pinion powered by the same tandem pump as ABC, with speed-sensitive assist.

sw221_tubs*.name: Wheel Tubs / Inner Arches
sw221_tubs*.function: Inner wheelhouse panels; keep spray and debris out of the bay.
sw221_tubs*.description: The front liners hide the ABC lines and headlamp washer plumbing.

sw221_radsupport*.name: Radiator Support
sw221_radsupport*.function: Front-end carrier for the cooling pack, latches and headlamps.
sw221_radsupport*.description: The bolt-on front module — after a front hit, this gets replaced along with the crash member.

w221_radiator*.name: Cooling Pack (Radiator)
w221_radiator*.function: Rejects engine heat to ambient air.
w221_radiator*.description: The S65 stacks the engine radiator with ATF, ABC/PS and low-temperature charge-cooling radiators — the whole nose is heat exchanger.

sw221_fueltank*.name: Fuel Tank
sw221_fueltank*.function: Stores 90 L; houses the in-tank pump and level senders.
sw221_fueltank*.description: Saddle tank over the rear axle with a jet pump balancing the halves. The V12 at full boost can empty it in well under 200 km.

sw221_driveshaft*.name: Propeller Shaft
sw221_driveshaft*.function: Carries torque from the transmission to the rear differential.
sw221_driveshaft*.description: Two-piece with centre bearing and flex discs. Cracked flex discs cause the classic driveline clunk.

sw221_halfshaft*.name: Axle Shaft (Halfshaft)
sw221_halfshaft*.function: Drives a rear wheel from the differential.
sw221_halfshaft*.description: CV-jointed shafts, uprated on the S65 for the 1,000 Nm torque cap.

sw221_diff*.name: Rear Differential
sw221_diff*.function: Splits torque between the rear wheels.
sw221_diff*.description: Strengthened centre with a shorter final drive than lesser W221s — it runs hot enough under load to need cooling fins.

sw221_exhausttip*.name: Quad Exhaust Tips
sw221_exhausttip*.function: Finishers where the twin pipes exit each side.
sw221_exhausttip*.description: Chromed twin oval tips per side — the S65 signature. V8 AMGs of the era wore squared tips.

sw221_exhaust_*.name: Exhaust System
sw221_exhaust_*.function: Carries exhaust through catalysts and mufflers to the rear.
sw221_exhaust_*.description: True dual system, one path per bank. Most of the V12's distant-thunder signature is made in the AMG mufflers.

sw221_bumperbar*.name: Front Crash Member
sw221_bumperbar*.function: Primary energy path in a frontal impact.
sw221_bumperbar*.description: Aluminium beam behind the bumper skin, bolted via crash boxes designed to crumple and be replaced.

sw221_engine*.name: M275 V12 Biturbo (in bay)
sw221_engine*.function: 6.0L twin-turbo V12 — 612 hp, 1,000 Nm. Use Focus Engine to study it in detail.
sw221_engine*.description: Sits longitudinally, set back against the firewall; the bay is so packed the turbos are barely visible from above. Switch to the engine focus view to peel it down to the crank.

sw221_transmission*.name: 5G-Tronic Transmission (W5A 780)
sw221_transmission*.function: Five-speed automatic rated for the V12's 1,000 Nm.
sw221_transmission*.description: The S65 kept the older 5-speed because the 7G-Tronic couldn't take the torque — strengthened W5A 780 with AMG Speedshift calibration.

sw221_intercooler*.name: Charge-air Intercooler
sw221_intercooler*.function: Cools compressed charge air before the intake.
sw221_intercooler*.description: Water-to-air system with its own low-temperature circuit and pump — compact and immune to heat soak at low road speed.

sw221_heatshield*.name: Heat Shield
sw221_heatshield*.function: Protects the tunnel and floor from exhaust heat.
sw221_heatshield*.description: Layered aluminium shields — safety-relevant on a car whose exhaust runs ~800 °C at the downpipes.

sw221_engbaycrap*.name: Engine Bay Ancillaries
sw221_engbaycrap*.function: Reservoirs, ducting and small hardware around the engine.
sw221_engbaycrap*.description: Coolant and ABC reservoirs, air ducts, covers — the clutter that makes real engine bays look nothing like brochures.

Eng_Block.name: Cylinder Block (Crankcase)
Eng_Block.function: Houses the crankshaft and both cylinder banks; the structural core of the engine.
Eng_Block.description: Aluminium block with cast-in Silitec liners, 60° bank angle, 5,980 cc. The 60° V12 is inherently balanced, which is why the M275 idles almost imperceptibly despite 612 hp in S65 tune.

Eng_CylinderBank_*.name: Cylinder Bank
Eng_CylinderBank_*.function: Carries six cylinders, one SOHC head and the cam drive for that side.
Eng_CylinderBank_*.description: Each bank has a single overhead cam operating three valves per cylinder (two intake, one exhaust) with twin spark plugs per cylinder — a Mercedes V12 signature of this era.

Eng_HeadCover_*.name: Cylinder Head Cover
Eng_HeadCover_*.function: Seals the valvetrain and carries the ignition coil packs.
Eng_HeadCover_*.description: Under each cover sit twelve coils (two per cylinder — twin-spark). Oil weep at these covers is a common M275 service item.

Eng_IntakeManifold.name: Intake Manifold
Eng_IntakeManifold.function: Distributes cooled, compressed charge air to all twelve cylinders.
Eng_IntakeManifold.description: Sits in the engine's V, fed by the water-to-air intercoolers. Downstream of both throttle bodies; runners are kept short because the turbos, not runner tuning, provide the torque.

Eng_Turbo_*.name: Turbocharger
Eng_Turbo_*.function: Uses exhaust energy to compress intake air, one turbo per bank.
Eng_Turbo_*.description: Two water-cooled turbochargers give the S65 tune ~1.5 bar of boost, 612 hp and a limited 1,000 Nm from 2,000 rpm. Listen for wastegate rattle on this engine family.

Eng_ExhaustManifold_*.name: Exhaust Manifold
Eng_ExhaustManifold_*.function: Collects exhaust from one bank and drives its turbocharger turbine.
Eng_ExhaustManifold_*.description: Short cast manifolds keep thermal mass low for fast turbo response. Heat management here is critical — the bay runs hot enough that heat shields are safety-relevant parts.

Eng_Intercooler_*.name: Charge-air Intercooler (water-to-air)
Eng_Intercooler_*.function: Cools compressed charge air before it enters the intake manifold.
Eng_Intercooler_*.description: The M275 uses a low-temperature liquid circuit with its own pump and front radiator instead of bulky air-to-air cores — compact enough to sit on top of the engine and immune to heat soak at low road speed.

Eng_ChargePipe_*.name: Charge Pipe
Eng_ChargePipe_*.function: Routes compressed air from turbo outlet to intercooler and manifold.
Eng_ChargePipe_*.description: Boost leaks at these joints cause soft low-end torque and rich-running codes — pressure-test here first when boost is down.

Eng_OilPan.name: Oil Pan (Sump)
Eng_OilPan.function: Reservoir for ~10 L of engine oil; feeds the oil pump pickup.
Eng_OilPan.description: The V12 holds significantly more oil than the V8s. Level sensing is electronic — there is no dipstick on late M275 cars.

Eng_Alternator.name: Alternator
Eng_Alternator.function: Generates electrical power for the 12 V system, belt-driven off the crank.
Eng_Alternator.description: High-output unit (~190 A) to feed the W221's dozens of ECUs. Buried low on the engine — access is from below with the belly pan removed.

Eng_ACCompressor.name: A/C Compressor
Eng_ACCompressor.function: Compresses refrigerant for the climate control system.
Eng_ACCompressor.description: Driven by the single serpentine belt. On the S65 the belt also drives the critical ABC tandem pump — a snapped belt disables suspension assist, steering assist and charging at once.

Eng_CrankPulley.name: Crankshaft Pulley (Damper)
Eng_CrankPulley.function: Drives the serpentine belt and damps crankshaft torsional vibration.
Eng_CrankPulley.description: A dual-mass elastomer damper. Inspect the rubber bond ring during belt service — separation causes belt misalignment and vibration.

Eng_FrontCover.name: Front Timing Cover
Eng_FrontCover.function: Encloses the timing chain drive at the front of the block.
Eng_FrontCover.description: Behind it, a duplex chain drives both bank camshafts. Chain stretch is rare on the M275 compared with later M157 V8s, but guide rails age with oil neglect.

Eng_SerpentineBelt.name: Serpentine Belt
Eng_SerpentineBelt.function: Single poly-V belt driving alternator, A/C compressor and the ABC tandem pump off the crank pulley.
Eng_SerpentineBelt.description: One belt runs everything — if it fails, the car loses charging, A/C, steering assist and ABC suspension pressure simultaneously. Inspect at every service.

Eng_ABCTandemPump.name: ABC / Power Steering Tandem Pump
Eng_ABCTandemPump.function: One pump body with two circuits: ~200 bar for Active Body Control plus the steering assist circuit.
Eng_ABCTandemPump.description: The hardest-worked ancillary on an S65. Whining at idle or a 'white steam' aerated reservoir means it's on the way out — and ABC struts drop soon after.

Eng_ThrottleBody_*.name: Throttle Body
Eng_ThrottleBody_*.function: Meters airflow into the intake plenum, one per bank.
Eng_ThrottleBody_*.description: Two electronic throttles, cross-checked by the ECU. Limp-home mode (fixed ~1,200 rpm) usually traces back to a throttle actuator fault.

Eng_Downpipe_*.name: Turbo Downpipe
Eng_Downpipe_*.function: Carries exhaust from the turbine outlet to the catalysts.
Eng_Downpipe_*.description: Post-turbine exhaust is still ~800 °C at full load; the downpipes are double-walled with an air gap for heat control.

Eng_OilFilter.name: Oil Filter Housing
Eng_OilFilter.function: Full-flow filtration of the ~10 L oil charge.
Eng_OilFilter.description: Top-loading cartridge filter — one of the few genuinely easy service items on this engine.

Int_Crankshaft.name: Crankshaft
Int_Crankshaft.function: Converts piston thrust into rotating torque delivered to the flywheel.
Int_Crankshaft.description: Forged steel, seven main bearings, six crankpins each shared by two connecting rods (one per bank). The 60° V12 fires every 60° of rotation — twelve evenly spaced power pulses per cycle is why the engine is this smooth.

Int_Piston_*.name: Piston
Int_Piston_*.function: Transfers combustion pressure to the connecting rod.
Int_Piston_*.description: Cast aluminium with a low 9.0:1 compression ratio to tolerate ~1.5 bar boost. Cooled by oil squirters aimed at the underside of the crown — a must at 1,000 Nm.

Int_ConnectingRod_*.name: Connecting Rod
Int_ConnectingRod_*.function: Links a piston to its crankpin, converting linear to rotary motion.
Int_ConnectingRod_*.description: Forged, cracked-cap steel rods. Two rods share each crankpin side by side, which is why opposing bank cylinders are slightly offset along the block.

Int_Camshaft_*.name: Camshaft (SOHC, one per bank)
Int_Camshaft_*.function: Opens the three valves per cylinder in time with the crank (2:1 reduction).
Int_Camshaft_*.description: The M275 is single-overhead-cam with 3 valves per cylinder — chosen for compact heads and fast catalyst light-off, not peak-rpm breathing. The turbos make up for the airflow. No variable valve timing on this engine.

Int_Valves_*.name: Valves (3 per cylinder)
Int_Valves_*.function: Two intake valves and one exhaust valve per cylinder admit charge air and release exhaust.
Int_Valves_*.description: Opened by roller rockers with hydraulic lash compensation — no adjustment ever needed. The single large exhaust valve runs a sodium-filled stem for cooling.

Int_TimingChain.name: Timing Chain (Duplex)
Int_TimingChain.function: Synchronises both camshafts to the crankshaft at a 2:1 reduction.
Int_TimingChain.description: A double-row chain runs up the front of the block over both cam sprockets, tensioned hydraulically. Rattle at cold start points at the tensioner or guide rails.

Int_CamSprocket_*.name: Camshaft Sprocket
Int_CamSprocket_*.function: Driven by the timing chain; turns the camshaft at half crank speed.
Int_CamSprocket_*.description: Bolted to the cam nose. The M275 has no cam phasers — valve timing is fixed, and boost does the breathing work.

Int_CrankSprocket.name: Crankshaft Sprocket
Int_CrankSprocket.function: Drives the timing chain from the crank nose.
Int_CrankSprocket.description: Keyed to the crankshaft in front of main bearing #1, behind the front cover and crank pulley.

Int_Flywheel.name: Flywheel / Drive Plate
Int_Flywheel.function: Smooths power delivery and couples the crank to the torque converter.
Int_Flywheel.description: A flex plate bolts the crank to the 5G-Tronic torque converter. Also carries the ring gear the starter engages and the crank position sensor's trigger teeth.
